violations.text: The cold hold unit at the sushi bar was observed held at 48 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Raw shrimp was tempted at 48 DF and was voluntarily disposed of. Establishment is to fix unit before resuming use Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/02/2025

Chicken was observed thawing on the counter. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. Chicken was observed at 80 DF. Chicken was voluntarily disposed of. New Violation. Correct By: 07/02/2025

The cold hold unit at the sushi bar was observed held at 48 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Raw Beef was tempted at 45 DF and moved to walk in cooler. Establishment is to fix unit before resuming use. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2025
Reduced oxygen packaged crab meat was observed thawing in an intact ROP package. Crab meat was tempted at 41 DF. Crab meat was voluntarily disposed of. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2025
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. New Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. No person trained as a Food Safety Manager was present during time of inspection. Establishment must have employees trained as Food Safety Managers. New Violation. Correct By: 08/05/2025

The hand-washing sink in the kitchen on the dish line was blocked by a hose. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. The hand washing sink in the kitchen on the cooking line was lacking paper towels. The hand washing sink at the sushi bar was lacking soap and paper towels. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2025

Raw animal foods (shrimp, beef and eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer, and ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Slicer was cleaned during time of inspection, Ice machine was emptied and turned off during time of inspection. Ice machine is to be professionally cleaned before being placed back in use. New Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2025

Chicken, noodles, and shrimp, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Food items were observed between 48-50DF. Food items were voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2025

Prep unit 1 was observed held at 50 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Chicken was observed at 50 DF, Egg rolls were observed at 50 DF. Fish was observed at 50 DF, Pork was observed at 50 DF, and Beef was observed at 50 DF. Food items were voluntarily disposed of. Prep unit is to remain out of service until, it is holding temperature of 41 DF or below. New Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2025

Food items prepared in advance were observed with out dates. Ready to eat temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 06/06/2025

Sushi rice was observed stored in Rice warmer from day before sushi rice was observed at 77 DF. Sushi rice was voluntarily disposed of. Establishment provided with Time as Public Health Control Request and logs. New Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of (Staff), Chicken, shrimp and noodles was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. Food items voluntarily disposed of. Proper cooling methods discussed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2025

Reduced oxygen packed tuna was observed thawing in an intact ROP package. Tuna was tempted at 41 DF. Tuna was voluntarily disposed of. When thawing ROP fish product it must be completely removed from package. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2025

Wet wiping cloths are not stored in a chemical sanitizer between uses. Wet wiping cloths must be stored in a chemical sanitizer at the appropriate concentration. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2025
Knifes were observed stored in between equipment between used. Utensils must be stored on a clean surface between uses. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2025
Dishware was not stored inverted at buffet line. Dishware is to be stored so that the food contact surface is facing down. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2025

The cutting boards in the kitchen are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the kitchen reach in coolers, between equipment, and cooking line have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces must be maintained. New Violation. Correct By: 06/07/2025
Water was observed gathering in a bucket, under the 3 bay sink. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2025
The hood system was observed with an excess of debris build up. The hood system must be maintained clean to prevent cross contamination. New Violation. Correct By: 06/07/2025

Raw salmon, raw tuna, cooked shrimp, spicy tuna, cooked salmon skin, seaweed salad, crab meat, lobster mixture was observed held at 49-53 degrees Fahrenheit. The ambient temperature of the two-door sushi lowboy was observed at 62*F. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Disposal was done. Unit taken out of service. New Violation. Correct By: 05/23/2023

Salmon skin, spicy tuna, lobster mixture, and seaweed salad no was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/23/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the interior of sushi low boy and rice cooker have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/25/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (a strainer and whisk stored on wall near the food prep sink) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2023
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2023

Wet wiping cloths are not stored in a chemical sanitizer between uses in the sushi area. Wet wiping cloths must be stored in a chemical sanitizer at the appropriate concentration. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2023

Avacados were not washed before being cut. Raw fruits and vegetables must be thoroughly washed in water to remove soil and other contaminants before they are cut. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the dry ingredient bins (rices, flour, etc.) have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. The inside of the chest freezer have an accumulation of frost and debris. Door track of one Pepsi refrigerator had an accumulation of debris and broken plastic. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2023

The walls near the food prep and 3-bay sink have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The ceiling and floor of the walk in freezer have an accumulation of ice and frozen condensate. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2023
